query: What's the maximum hotel rate for a business trip to New York?
ground_truth: CRITICAL UPDATE: Per the Q2 budget review email from Finance & Operations, the maximum nightly hotel rate has been temporarily reduced to $200/night for all city tiers, down from the standing policy of $250. No exceptions without CFO approval. This override is effective through end of Q3 2026. Standing policy: $250/night domestic, preferred vendors Marriott ($220), Hilton ($210), IHG ($190). Airbnb and vacation rentals are not permitted.

query: Can I book business class for my upcoming 12-hour flight to Tokyo?
ground_truth: CRITICAL UPDATE: Per the Q2 budget review, business class has been suspended for all routes. Premium economy is the maximum class until further notice. All international travel now requires CEO approval. Standing policy: business class requires VP approval for flights over 10 hours. Tokyo (12 hours) would normally qualify but business class is currently suspended. Preferred airline: Delta. Book at least 5 business days in advance.

query: How much can I spend on meals per day during a domestic US trip?
ground_truth: CRITICAL UPDATE: Per the Q2 budget review, the daily per diem has been reduced to $60/day and the GSA schedule is temporarily suspended through Q3 2026. Standing policy: $75/day meal limit. Alcohol not reimbursable unless client dinner with prior approval. Receipts required for expenses over $25. Submit within 10 business days of trip completion.

query: What approval do I need for an international business trip?
ground_truth: CRITICAL UPDATE: Per the Q2 budget review, all international travel now requires CEO approval regardless of duration, changed from VP level. Standing policy: VP approval for international travel. Additional requirements include security briefings, valid travel documents, visa and insurance. International flights must be booked at least 10 business days in advance.
